Object.Name: LAN Configuration
Object.Description1: LAN network configuration. DHCP server settings, IP ranges, DNS relay, and client lease management.

Item.Name: LAN IP Address
Item.Description: IPv4 address of the LAN interface (e.g., "192.168.1.1"). This is the router's IP address on the local network and serves as the default gateway for LAN clients. Must be a valid IPv4 address in dotted-decimal notation.

Item.Name: Subnet Mask
Item.Description: Subnet mask for the LAN network in dotted-decimal notation (e.g., "255.255.255.0" for a /24 network). Defines the size of the local network and which IP addresses are considered part of the LAN segment. Must be a valid subnet mask with contiguous 1 bits.

Item.Name: DHCP Enabled
Item.Description: Enable or disable the DHCP server on this LAN interface. When enabled (true), the router will automatically assign IP addresses to clients from the configured DHCP pool. When disabled (false), clients must use static IP configuration.

Item.Name: DHCP Pool Start
Item.Description: Starting IP address of the DHCP address pool (e.g., "192.168.1.100"). This is the first IP address that can be dynamically assigned to DHCP clients. Must be within the LAN subnet and not conflict with the router's IP or static assignments.

Item.Name: DHCP Pool End
Item.Description: Ending IP address of the DHCP address pool (e.g., "192.168.1.250"). This is the last IP address that can be dynamically assigned to DHCP clients. Must be within the LAN subnet and greater than or equal to the pool start address.

Item.Name: DHCP Lease Time
Item.Description: Duration in seconds for which a DHCP lease remains valid (range: 60-604800 seconds / 1 minute to 7 days). After this time, clients must renew their lease or obtain a new IP address. Common values: 43200 (12 hours), 86400 (24 hours). Shorter lease times allow for more dynamic network management but increase DHCP traffic.

Item.Name: DNS Server Primary
Item.Description: Primary DNS server IP address provided to DHCP clients (e.g., "8.8.8.8" for Google DNS). Clients will use this server for domain name resolution. Can be set to the router's IP if it provides DNS caching/forwarding, or to an external DNS service.

Item.Name: DNS Server Secondary
Item.Description: Secondary/fallback DNS server IP address provided to DHCP clients (e.g., "8.8.4.4"). Used by clients when the primary DNS server is unavailable. Provides redundancy for DNS resolution.

Item.Name: Domain Name
Item.Description: Local domain name suffix for the LAN (e.g., "lan", "home.local"). This domain is appended to hostnames for local name resolution and provided to DHCP clients. Allows clients to resolve local hostnames like "device.lan" instead of using IP addresses.

Item.Name: Static Leases
Item.Description: Static DHCP lease reservations in MAC:IP format (e.g., "00:11:22:33:44:55:192.168.1.50"). Associates a specific MAC address with a fixed IP address, ensuring that a device always receives the same IP. Useful for servers, printers, and other devices requiring consistent addressing. Multiple instances can be added for different devices.

Item.Name: Active Leases
Item.Description: Currently active DHCP leases in MAC:IP:hostname:expiry format (e.g., "00:11:22:33:44:55:192.168.1.100:laptop:3600"). Read-only resource that shows all devices currently holding DHCP leases, including their MAC address, assigned IP, hostname (if available), and remaining lease time in seconds. Automatically updated from the DHCP server's lease database.

Item.Name: Apply Configuration
Item.Description: Execute this resource to apply the current LAN and DHCP configuration to the system. Writes all configured settings to OpenWRT's UCI configuration files (/etc/config/network and /etc/config/dhcp), commits the changes, and restarts the network and dnsmasq services to activate the new configuration. After execution, the Configuration Status resource will indicate success or failure.

Item.Name: Configuration Status
Item.Description: Current status of the LAN configuration: 0=Pending (configuration modified but not yet applied), 1=Applied (configuration successfully applied to the system), 2=Error (last configuration apply attempt failed). Check this resource after executing Apply Configuration to verify successful application.
